zh.lifeSummary: 當William Mason I於1428年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾邱奇 斯特頓時，他的父親Richard L Mason IV26歲，母親Johanna Short22歲。他於1453年與Alice Dunne or Done在英國英格蘭史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1489年在英國史洛普爾享年61歲。

nameMeanings.name: Mason
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English masoun ‘mason, stone worker, builder in stone’ (Old Central French maçon, masson). A Middle English form machun (derived from Old French machun) gives rise to Machin and its variants. See also Mayson . Stonemasonry was a hugely important craft in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Masson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Lucien, Alain, Henri, Pierre, Yves, Agathe, Aime, Cecile, Clovis, Francois, Francoise.

variants.name: Machin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Juan, Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Mario, Miguel, Pedro, Blanca, Diego, Luis, Ramon.
English: variant of   Machen  . This is the more common form of the name in Britain and Ireland.
Spanish (Machín): probably a nickname from machín ‘boor, lout’, often applied to a blacksmith's apprentice.

variants.name: Macon
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places so called, e.g. in Saône-et-Loire.
French: from the old oblique form of the ancient Germanic personal name Mako, a variant of Mago, a short form of compound names formed with maht ‘strength, power’ as the first element.
French (Maçon): occupational name for a mason, (Old) French maçon.

variants.name: Machen
meanings: English: variant of Mason , from the Middle English form machun (derived from Old French machun).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When William Mason I was born in 1428, in Church Stretton, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard L Mason IV, was 26 and his mother, Johanna Short, was 22. He married Alice Dunne or Done in 1453, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1489, in Shropshire, England, at the age of 61.

ko.Saint Laurence's Church, Church Stretton: 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 처르치 스트레턴 스트 로렌스'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: William Mason I(이)가 1428년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 처르치 스트레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard L Mason IV(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Johanna Short(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1453년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 Alice Dunne or Done(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1489년 영국 슈럽실르에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.
